title: Add PID guard to launch_all.py to prevent duplicate launcher instances
objective: launch_all.py has no guard against being run while already running. On 2026-04-04, the user ran it multiple times. Each invocation spawned a new bot_core.py and recommendation_api.py. The watchdog inside each launcher also restarted children independently. Result: 6 bot_core.py + 3 launch_all.py + 10 recommendation_api.py running simultaneously. Fix: on startup, write a launcher PID file and refuse to start if another launcher PID is already alive.

required_changes.1_launcher_pid_file: On startup, check for runtime/launcher.pid. If it exists, read the PID. If that PID is still alive (use os.kill(pid, 0) — raises OSError if not running), print an error and sys.exit(1). If PID is dead or file missing, write current PID to runtime/launcher.pid.
required_changes.2_cleanup_on_exit: In the finally/cleanup block (KeyboardInterrupt or normal exit), delete runtime/launcher.pid.
required_changes.3_no_other_changes: Do not change the watchdog logic, subprocess start logic, or any other file.

pseudocode: os.kill(old_pid, 0)  # raises OSError if not running
print(f'ERROR: launcher already running at PID {old_pid}. Exiting.')
pass  # old PID is dead, safe to continue
acceptance_criteria: Running launch_all.py once: starts normally, writes runtime/launcher.pid
Running launch_all.py a second time while first is alive: prints error and exits immediately without spawning any children
After the first launcher is stopped (Ctrl+C), runtime/launcher.pid is deleted
If runtime/launcher.pid exists with a dead PID (e.g. system restart), launcher starts normally
No other behavior changes — watchdog, child processes, log files unchanged
verification: python launch_all.py  # second terminal: should print error and exit
Ctrl+C the first: runtime/launcher.pid should be gone
notes: os.kill(pid, 0) works on Windows with Python — raises OSError if process not found.
missing_ok=True on Path.unlink() requires Python 3.8+ — acceptable given project uses 3.14.
runtime/ directory already exists (bot.pid is written there by bot_core.py).
